articles.headline: Pakistan airstrike kills 400 at Kabul drug rehab centre
articles.summary: More than 400 people were killed and 265 wounded in a Pakistani airstrike on a drug rehabilitation centre in Kabul. The Taliban government called it the deadliest incident since border fighting began in October. Pakistan rejected the claims, stating it precisely targeted military installations and terrorist infrastructure rather than civilian hospitals.

articles.headline: Ukraine protests arrest of six nationals in India for drone training
articles.summary: Ukraine has lodged a formal protest with New Delhi following the arrest of six of its nationals by the NIA. Investigative agencies claim the individuals entered Myanmar illegally via Mizoram to train ethnic insurgent groups in drone warfare. The NIA is also searching for eight other Ukrainians believed to be part of the same operation.

articles.headline: Gulf nations urge US to fully degrade Iran's military capacity
articles.summary: Gulf Arab states are reportedly pressing the US to permanently neutralize Iran's military capabilities to protect regional oil lifelines. While initially opposing the war, drone and missile strikes on UAE and Saudi infrastructure have shifted the mood. The Trump administration is seeking regional backing to bolster the international legitimacy of the war.

articles.headline: China to provide emergency humanitarian aid to West Asian nations
articles.summary: Beijing announced it will provide emergency aid to Iran, Lebanon, Jordan, and Iraq to alleviate the humanitarian crisis caused by the ongoing regional conflict. China expressed hope that the assistance will ease the plight of local people affected by airstrikes and supply chain disruptions while calling for diplomatic solutions.

articles.headline: Trump postpones China trip as West Asia war upends diplomacy
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump has postponed his highly anticipated visit to Beijing to meet President Xi Jinping by five to six weeks. The delay is attributed to the escalating war with Iran, which has driven oil prices higher and focused US attention on energy security and the Strait of Hormuz blockade.

articles.headline: Trump vows to 'take' Cuba as island suffers total power blackout
articles.summary: President Trump stated his intention to 'take' or 'free' Cuba as the nation grapples with a total electrical grid collapse linked to a US oil embargo. The island’s 9.6 million people are facing daily outages of up to 20 hours. Trump described Cuba as a 'weakened nation' and suggested the US is ready to intervene after decades of defiance.

articles.headline: War could push 45 million people into acute hunger
articles.summary: The World Food Programme warns that 45 million more people could face acute hunger by June if the Iran war persists. US-Israeli attacks have blocked key aid routes, causing a spike in food, oil, and shipping costs that delays life-saving shipments to global crisis zones.

articles.headline: US National Counterterrorism Centre head resigns over Iran war
articles.summary: Joseph Kent, head of the US National Counterterrorism Centre, resigned in protest against the war in Iran. Kent stated that Tehran posed no imminent threat and that the conflict was initiated due to pressure from external lobbies. He is the most senior Trump administration official to step down since the war began three weeks ago.

articles.headline: Pakistan Airstrikes Kill 400 at Kabul Hospital; Afghanistan Vows Response
articles.summary: The Taliban government said 400 people were killed by Pakistani airstrikes on the Omid Addiction Treatment Hospital in Kabul. Pakistan denied targeting civilians, stating the strikes hit military infrastructure. India’s foreign ministry condemned the “barbaric” act. The escalation follows border clashes between the two neighbors that erupted last month.

articles.headline: Lebanon Proposes First Direct Talks With Israel to End Fighting
articles.summary: Lebanon’s government has broken a decades-long taboo by proposing direct talks with Israel as Israeli troops advance and airstrikes hit Beirut. Lebanese officials seek an end to the fighting with Hezbollah. Meanwhile, EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas warned of food and energy crises if diplomatic ways to keep the Strait of Hormuz open are not found.

articles.headline: Pakistani airstrike on Kabul hospital kills over 400 people
articles.summary: A Pakistani airstrike destroyed the Omid Addiction Treatment Hospital in Kabul, killing 408 people and injuring 265. Afghanistan called the attack a violation of sovereignty, while Pakistan claimed it targeted a military facility. The strike marks a major escalation in the three-week conflict. India condemned the act as barbaric and a threat to regional peace.

articles.headline: Suicide attacks in Maiduguri kill 23 and injure over 100
articles.summary: Coordinated explosions by suspected suicide bombers hit a busy market in the Nigerian city of Maiduguri, killing at least 23 people and wounding 108. The military attributed the blasts to Boko Haram militants. The attacks occurred shortly after residents broke their Ramadan fast, causing widespread panic in the northeastern city.

articles.headline: UN warns of ethnic cleansing in occupied West Bank settlements
articles.summary: The U.N. human rights office warned of possible ethnic cleansing in the West Bank, citing the forced displacement of over 36,000 Palestinians in one year. A report by Volker Turk's office denounced the acceleration of Israeli settlements and daily raids that impede access to health and education. The U.N. accused Israeli authorities of directing or enabling settler violence.

articles.headline: Nirav Modi moves UK High Court in final bid against extradition
articles.summary: Fugitive diamantaire Nirav Modi has applied to the UK High Court to reopen his extradition appeal, citing new developments and the risk of ill-treatment in Indian custody. India has opposed the move, arguing that sovereign assurances regarding his safety are binding and that the application is a delay tactic. The court has reserved its judgment.

articles.headline: Centre seeks executive power to define sovereign functions in SC
articles.summary: The central government told a nine-judge Constitution Bench that the executive should have the authority to decide what constitutes a 'sovereign function' and, by extension, what is an 'industry' under labor laws. The Centre argued that welfare schemes should not be treated as industrial activity, even if they resemble commercial operations.

articles.headline: SC directs Centre to set policy for bank accounts of deceased
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has asked the Centre and RBI to formulate a policy to allow legal heirs to easily access details of bank accounts held by deceased persons. Hearing a PIL, the court noted that families often face significant hurdles in identifying dormant accounts and unclaimed deposits when no nomination or KYC updates were made.

articles.headline: SIT formed to probe Telangana farmhouse drugs and arms case
articles.summary: The Telangana government constituted a nine-member Special Investigation Team to investigate a drug and arms case involving former MLA Panjugula Rohith Reddy. The case follows a raid on a farmhouse where drugs were allegedly consumed and a shot was fired. Eleven people, including an Andhra MP, were previously detained.

articles.headline: Delhi HC orders removal of posts linking Puri's daughter to Epstein
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court ordered social media platforms to remove defamatory content within 24 hours that linked Himayani Puri, daughter of Union Minister Hardeep Singh Puri, to sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. The court mandated a takedown for content uploaded from Indian IP addresses and blocking of international access within India.

articles.headline: SC Strikes Down Curbs on Maternity Benefits for Adoptive Mothers
articles.summary: The Supreme Court set aside a legal provision that restricted maternity benefits to mothers who adopt children younger than three months old. Ruling the restriction arbitrary and violative of the right to equality, the bench stated that an adoptive child's needs are identical to a biological child's. The court also urged the Centre to introduce legislation recognizing paternity leave.

articles.headline: High Courts Increase Scrutiny Over Coercive GST Enforcement Actions
articles.summary: High Courts in Bombay and Punjab & Haryana have set aside GST registration cancellations and bank account attachments where authorities failed to follow due process. The courts ruled that authorities must establish fraudulent intent (mens rea) and provide a proper personal hearing rather than passing non-speaking orders based on vague notices.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Calls for Centre-State Board to Oversee Labour Codes
articles.summary: The Standing Committee on Labour recommended a permanent Centre-State Coordination board to oversee the rollout of four new Labour Codes. The panel noted poor performance by some states in implementing central schemes and urged the mandatory registration of gig workers on the e-Shram portal. The government is expected to notify the codes by the end of March 2026.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Directs Centre to Enact Legal Framework for Paternity Leave
articles.summary: The Supreme Court called on the Union government to recognize paternity leave as a component of social security to promote gender equality and balanced childcare. The court ruled that adoptive mothers are entitled to 12 weeks of maternity leave regardless of the child’s age, reading down a provision that restricted benefits to adoptions of infants below three months.

articles.headline: Karnataka High Court seeks commercial LPG stock details from government
articles.summary: The High Court of Karnataka directed the State and Union governments to provide details of commercial LPG stocks by March 23. The order follows a petition by the Bangalore Hotels’ Association against a drastic reduction in supply. Current government orders restrict supply to only 7,000 cylinders per day for the entire state, with only 1,000 earmarked for the hospitality sector.

articles.headline: Techie loses 14.6 lakh in Bengaluru credit card protection fraud
articles.summary: A 40-year-old techie from Mahadevapura was duped of ₹14.6 lakh after clicking a malicious link sent by fraudsters posing as bank executives. The victim was told her credit card protection plan needed deactivation. Multiple unauthorized transactions occurred once her phone was compromised. Police have registered a case under the IT Act and Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita.

articles.headline: High Court refuses to quash rape case against guest teacher
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court rejected a petition to quash a rape case against a 47-year-old government teacher accused of sexually abusing a guest teacher under duress for over a year. The court noted that the allegations depict submission extracted via threats of professional ruin rather than consent. The petitioner must now face a full-fledged trial.

articles.headline: Karnataka government plans new Excise Act to curb illegal liquor
articles.summary: Excise Minister R.B. Thimmapur informed the Assembly that the government is considering a new law to replace the Karnataka Excise Act, 1965. The proposal includes bringing illegal liquor sales under the Narcotic Drugs and Psychotropic Substances (NDPS) Act for stricter enforcement. The move follows reports of rampant illegal liquor sales in grocery stores in Raichur district.

articles.headline: AFT stays retirement of Colonel Purohit pending promotion complaint
articles.summary: The Armed Forces Tribunal (AFT) stayed the retirement of Colonel Shrikant Prasad Purohit, who was acquitted in the 2008 Malegaon blast case. Purohit sought promotion and service benefits, arguing his career was stalled by the prolonged trial and arrest. The tribunal directed that his retirement be kept in abeyance until his statutory complaint is decided.

articles.headline: NIA probes foreign nationals for training ethnic groups in Myanmar
articles.summary: The NIA is investigating a network involving U.S. and Ukrainian nationals arrested for allegedly crossing into Myanmar to train armed ethnic groups in drone warfare and weapons handling. Six foreigners were apprehended at various Indian airports. Digital footprints suggest involvements in covert operations. Ukraine’s embassy has requested consular access and denied illegal activity claims.

articles.headline: Five-year prison terms awarded in 2016 Una Dalit flogging case
articles.summary: A special court in Gujarat sentenced five men to five years in prison for the 2016 flogging of four Dalit men in Una. The victims were assaulted while attempting to skin a dead cow. The court also imposed a ₹5,000 fine on each convict. The verdict comes years after the incident sparked nationwide protests over Dalit rights and safety.

articles.headline: Noel Tata seeks two-year extension for Tata Sons chairman Chandrasekaran
articles.summary: Tata Trusts chairman Noel Tata is reportedly in favor of a two-year extension for N. Chandrasekaran instead of a full five-year term. The move is intended to facilitate a leadership overhaul and succession plan. The Tata Sons board has deferred a decision as trustees seek clearer performance benchmarks for new ventures like e-commerce and aviation.

articles.headline: Jio Platforms close to filing for India’s biggest private IPO
articles.summary: Reliance Industries' telecom arm, Jio Platforms, is expected to file its IPO papers within three weeks. The company targets a valuation between $100 billion and $120 billion. The offer will involve a 2.5% stake sale, following recent government rule changes that lowered the minimum public shareholding requirement for very large companies.

articles.headline: Canadian billionaire Stephen Smith buys 26.9% stake in The Economist
articles.summary: Mortgage billionaire Stephen Smith has agreed to acquire a minority stake in The Economist Group from the Rothschild family. The deal marks the first major ownership change for the British publication in a decade. Smith joins the Agnelli, Cadbury, and Schroder families as owners of the digital-first current affairs magazine.

articles.headline: Indian cotton yarn demand rises in China, Bangladesh, and Vietnam
articles.summary: Demand for Indian cotton yarn has increased in China, Bangladesh, and Vietnam due to global logistics disruptions from the West Asia conflict. Cotton futures on the ICE have risen by 13% over two weeks, while domestic yarn prices have improved by ₹10-₹15 per kg. The Cotton Corporation of India has increased prices by ₹1,200 per candy to manage tight supply conditions.

articles.headline: Air India restores all 30 long-grounded legacy aircraft to service
articles.summary: Air India has successfully brought back into service all 30 legacy aircraft that were grounded at the time of its 2022 privatisation. The restored fleet includes 19 narrowbody and 11 widebody planes, bringing the total fleet strength to 186. The final aircraft, a Boeing 777-300ER (VT-ALL), returned to service following a comprehensive nose-to-tail restoration and regulatory validation.

articles.headline: Andhra Petrochemicals suspends operations at Visakhapatnam plant
articles.summary: Andhra Petrochemicals has halted production at its Vizag facility following a disruption in propylene supply from HPCL. The raw material shortage is linked to the ongoing conflict in West Asia. The company will resume operations once the supply chain stabilizes.

articles.headline: VinFast extends free electric vehicle charging programme until 2029
articles.summary: Vietnamese EV maker VinFast has extended its free charging drive for customers in India, Indonesia, Thailand, and the Philippines through March 2029. The initiative aims to accelerate EV adoption in Southeast Asia and the Indian market by reducing ownership costs for early adopters.

articles.headline: CEAT to invest ₹1,300 crore to expand Chennai radial plant
articles.summary: Tyre manufacturer CEAT plans to invest a fresh ₹1,300 crore in its Sriperumbudur facility near Chennai. The expansion will add 35 lakh passenger car radial tyres annually, bringing total capacity to 1.4 crore units. The project is expected to be operational by the first half of FY28 and will further establish the site as a key export hub for Europe and the Middle East.

articles.headline: Hotel Leela acquires luxury Coorg resort for ₹560 crore
articles.summary: Leela Palaces Hotels & Resorts has acquired a 71-villa luxury resort in Coorg, marking its entry into the nature-led wellness segment. The property, spread across 76 acres, is expected to generate annual revenue of ₹165–175 crore. The company plans to add 19 more villas in the first phase, bringing the total inventory to 90.

articles.headline: FTAs expected to drive 7% growth in luxury car market
articles.summary: Audi India expects the domestic luxury car market to grow by 5-7% this year, aided by India's recent free trade agreements. However, Brand Director Balbir Singh Dhillon noted that currency fluctuations remain a challenge, with the Euro's appreciation against the Rupee impacting costs. 95% of Audi cars sold in India are locally manufactured.

articles.headline: Bajaj Electricals acquires Morphy Richards brand rights for ₹141 crore
articles.summary: Bajaj Electricals has signed a ₹141.4 crore deal to acquire the intellectual property and brand rights for Morphy Richards in India and select South Asian markets. The company, which has licensed the brand since 2002, aims to use the full ownership to innovate faster and invest consistently in the premium home appliance segment.

articles.headline: Renault India ready for EV launch pending market demand
articles.summary: Renault India is technically prepared to launch its first electric vehicle but will wait for consumer penetration to exceed the current 4% of the car market. The company is focusing on the all-new Duster launch with hybrid options first. Production for the upcoming Bridger EV concept is expected to start next year.

articles.headline: CBIC simplifies back-to-town clearance for diverted export cargo
articles.summary: The Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs has directed all Custom Houses to adopt a uniform procedure for export containers returned to Indian ports other than their original port of departure. This back-to-town (BTT) procedure provides major relief to exporters whose shipments were stranded or diverted due to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.headline: States lag in allocating commercial LPG for hospitality sector
articles.summary: Industry bodies NRAI and FHRAI report that many states, including Maharashtra, have yet to announce commercial LPG allocations for restaurants despite central directives. Supply constraints are forcing some iconic eateries to shut down or shorten hours. Up to 5% of organized restaurants have reportedly suspended operations due to the fuel crisis.

articles.headline: Innovision IPO receives 3.32 times subscription on final day
articles.summary: The initial public offering of toll plaza manager Innovision was subscribed 3.32 times, led by institutional demand (13.75x) and non-institutional investors (8.26x). Cautious retail sentiment saw that segment subscribed only 0.58 times. The company had extended the issue and lowered its price band to ₹494-519 to attract investors.

articles.headline: Indian equity indices gain as volatility index sharpens decline
articles.summary: The Nifty 50 and Sensex rose for a second session, gaining 0.74% and 0.75% respectively. Analysts noted that a sharp decline in India VIX (down 8.39%) helped stabilize the market after recent war-related turbulence. However, market breadth remained cautious as 440 stocks hit 52-week lows. IT stocks underperformed, while metals and auto sectors led the gains.

articles.headline: Macro volatility prompts start-ups to delay IPO plans
articles.summary: Several high-profile Indian start-ups, including PhonePe, Zepto, and OYO, are deferring their public listings due to geopolitical tensions and valuation mismatches. Founders are shifting focus toward profitability and unit economics as public market investors show zero tolerance for cash burn. Secondary sales and strategic rounds are emerging as alternative liquidity options.

articles.headline: Actively-managed mid and small-cap funds outperform in 2025
articles.summary: The SPIVA India 2025 report reveals that while 75% of large-cap funds underperformed their benchmarks, mid- and small-cap funds saw their best relative results since 2014. Only 12% of mid-cap funds failed to beat their index. Over a 10-year period, however, 76% of all actively managed equity funds have lagged behind their respective benchmarks.

articles.headline: Speciality Medicines to launch ₹29-crore BSE SME IPO
articles.summary: Pharmaceutical formulation firm Speciality Medicines plans to raise ₹29 crore through an IPO on the BSE SME platform. The issue, opening on March 24, will offer 23.5 lakh shares in a price band of ₹117-124. Proceeds will be used for expansion, working capital, and general corporate purposes.

articles.headline: Government warns insurance companies against unfair business practices
articles.summary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman informed the Rajya Sabha that the regulator is closely monitoring insurance firms for misrepresentation and suitability failures. In FY24, penalties were levied on Reliance General (₹2 crore) and Bajaj Finance (₹2 crore) for unfair practices. She also announced that 25,000 gram panchayats will be covered under new insurance inclusion rules.

articles.headline: Iran-US conflict disrupts Indian oilmeal exports to Europe
articles.summary: The Solvent Extractors’ Association reports that escalating tensions have forced shipping detours around the Cape of Good Hope, adding 15 days to transit times. Roughly 20% of Indian oilmeal exports destined for West Asia and 15% for Europe are at risk. Higher insurance premiums and costlier logistics are making Indian soybean meal less competitive globally.

articles.headline: L Catterton plans larger India fund to target consumer sector
articles.summary: Private equity firm L Catterton has raised $200 million of its $400-million India-focused fund and plans to raise the remainder by next year. The firm is betting on India's long-term consumer story, with portfolio brands like Drools and Haldiram's potentially heading for public listings. Revenues for its India portfolio companies grew by 25% over the past year.

articles.headline: New IPOs Increasingly Used for Debt Repayment over Growth
articles.summary: Data from Prime Database shows Indian companies are using IPO proceeds for deleveraging instead of funding expansion. Nearly Rs 35,055 crore has been allocated for debt repayment in 2024-2026, accounting for almost 24% of total funds raised. This shift suggests companies are utilizing a bullish equity market to repair balance sheets.

articles.headline: Purvah Green Withdraws Rs 5,750 Crore Bid for GVK Energy
articles.summary: Purvah Green, an RPSG firm, has withdrawn its resolution plan to acquire GVK Energy, citing uncertainties from the ongoing West Asia conflict. GVK Energy is currently undergoing insolvency with claims of Rs 14,281 crore. The Adani Group had submitted the second-highest bid of Rs 5,725 crore, placing them as a potential lead in the process.

articles.headline: Oil Refiners End Credit for Petrol Pump Dealers
articles.summary: State-run oil companies have withdrawn the standard 3-5 days of credit for petrol pump dealers, demanding same-day payment for fuel consignments. The move follows rising global crude prices and domestic losses for refiners. Dealers are now shortening credit periods for their own customers, such as large diesel buyers, as they struggle with cash flow management.

articles.headline: SpiceJet Offers Property Deed as Security in Maran Case
articles.summary: Facing a liquidity crunch, SpiceJet offered the Delhi High Court a title deed for an unencumbered property worth Rs 148 crore as security. This comes after the Supreme Court refused to interfere with an order to deposit Rs 144 crore cash in an arbitration dispute with former promoter Kalanithi Maran. SpiceJet cited losses from West Asia flight cancellations as the reason for the crunch.

articles.headline: JSW Kalinga Steel to Raise Rs 9,500 Crore via Bonds
articles.summary: JSW Kalinga Steel plans to raise Rs 9,500 crore through zero-coupon non-convertible debentures to finance a joint venture with Japan’s JFE Steel. The funds will support the Bhushan Power & Steel business acquired via insolvency. The bonds, rated AA by Crisil, will be issued in two tranches and redeemed at a premium in March 2031 with support from parent guarantees.

articles.headline: Cable and Wire Stocks Fall Amid Metal Price Surge
articles.summary: Shares of major cable and wire companies like Polycab and KEI Industries dropped 6-17% in March due to a spike in copper and aluminium prices. While demand remains steady, rising input costs and production constraints from LPG shortages have weighed on investor sentiment. Analysts expect mid-single-digit volume expansion as price adjustments usually occur with a lag.

articles.headline: Private Credit Market Shows Signs of Stress and Defaults
articles.summary: India's $25-billion private credit market is witnessing early signs of stress with reports of defaults around Rs 100 crore each. Due to 'light-touch' regulations, funds are not required to disclose asset-level performance, raising transparency concerns. High-yield investors are being advised to remain cautious as liquidity pressures and deteriorating borrower quality emerge.

articles.headline: BHIM App Targets 5% Market Share to Break UPI Duopoly
articles.summary: NPCI is positioning its BHIM application as a sovereign alternative to private players like PhonePe and Google Pay, which control nearly 80% of the UPI market. The goal is to reach a 5% market share over the next three years. BHIM currently holds a 0.86% share. NPCI aims to enforce a 30% market share cap for individual players by the end of 2026.

articles.headline: Investment Bankers Urge SEBI to Widen Block Deal Price Band
articles.summary: Investment bankers have approached SEBI seeking to widen the price band for block deals to 5%, up from the current 3%. Bankers argue that greater flexibility is needed to execute large transactions during periods of high stock price volatility. The current framework, revised in 2025, also set the minimum order size for block deals at Rs 25 crore.

articles.headline: NCLT Clears Adani Group’s Rs 15,000 Crore Resolution Plan for JAL
articles.summary: The Allahabad Bench of the NCLT approved Adani Enterprises’ Rs 15,000 crore resolution plan for Jaiprakash Associates Ltd (JAL), dismissing objections by Vedanta. Adani’s plan received 93% creditor approval. JAL entered insolvency in 2024 with debts exceeding Rs 55,000 crore. The plan includes an upfront payout of Rs 6,000 crore to creditors.

articles.headline: L Catterton India Plans Seven New Consumer Investments by 2027
articles.summary: L Catterton India has raised $200 million in the past year and aims to deploy $300 million by the end of 2027. The fund has a target size of up to $600 million including a greenshoe option. Partnering with former HUL Chairman Sanjiv Mehta, the fund targets the Indian consumption story with recent investments in Haldiram’s, Farmley, and Healing Hands Clinic.

articles.headline: Licious Targets 120 Micro Markets for 30-Minute Deliveries
articles.summary: D2C meat platform Licious is focusing on deep penetration in 120 urban micro markets rather than wide geographic expansion. The company plans to reach 400 dark stores to support its 30-minute delivery goal. Revenue from specific hubs like Whitefield has doubled within a year. Licious expects to close the financial year with Rs 1,100 crore in revenue.

articles.headline: Helmet Manufacturers Warn of Safety Risks Due to Rising Costs
articles.summary: The Helmet Manufacturers Association of India warned that a 74% spike in Expanded Polystyrene (EPS) prices and higher freight costs may force firms to reduce product quality. Global supply disruptions following military strikes on Iran have triggered the price surge. The association noted that lower material density could weaken the protective capability of helmets.

articles.headline: Renault Launches New Duster SUV in India at Rs 10.49 Lakh
articles.summary: Renault India has relaunched the Duster brand with prices for the turbo petrol version starting at Rs 10.49 lakh. The vehicle is built on the Renault Group Modular Platform (RGMP) and features 17 ADAS functions, dual-zone climate control, and integrated Google Automotive Services. Dispatches for the five available trims have commenced nationwide.

articles.headline: Audi India Head Says FTA Duties to Determine Future Lineup
articles.summary: Audi India Brand Director Balbir Singh Dhillon said the company will recalibrate its product launches once specific details of import duty cuts under the India-EU Free Trade Agreement are revealed. The agreement could slash duties from 110% to 10% under a quota system. The luxury market saw industry volumes remain flat at 52,000 units in 2025 due to rising commodity costs.

articles.headline: Plastic Processing Units Face Closure as Polymer Prices Surge 70%
articles.summary: Over 50% of plastic processing units in states like Odisha have halted production as polymer costs increased by up to 70% following the West Asia conflict. Polypropylene prices rose from Rs 1 lakh to over Rs 1.7 lakh per tonne. Manufacturers warn they cannot pass on costs due to existing supply contracts, threatening the livelihood of 5 million people employed in the sector.

articles.headline: India Revives Plans to Establish Domestic Maritime Insurance Entity
articles.summary: The government has revived efforts to set up a domestic Protection and Indemnity (P&I) entity as the West Asia crisis drives up marine insurance costs for exporters. Currently, around 300,000 tonnes of LPG cargo is stuck west of the Strait of Hormuz. A study on the entity’s regulatory and financial framework is nearing completion with the Ministry of Ports and Shipping.

articles.headline: SBI Raises Rs 6,051 Crore via Tier II Bonds at 7.05%
articles.summary: State Bank of India raised Rs 6,051 crore through its second Basel III compliant Tier 2 bond issuance of the fiscal year. The 10-year bonds, which have a call option after 5 years, attracted bids worth Rs 10,000 crore, double the base issue size. Participants included provident funds, pension funds, and mutual funds.

articles.headline: Bima Sugam to Launch Standardized Life and Health Insurance Products
articles.summary: The insurance industry will begin developing standardized motor, health, and life products for the Bima Sugam platform between July and September. Irdai also proposed a Public Insurance Repository (PIR) to modernize data infrastructure. The repository aims to provide consent-based access to structured insurance data, enhancing transparency and aiding underwriting.

articles.headline: Domestic LPG production surges 38% to curb supply shortages
articles.summary: India's domestic LPG production has increased to over 48,000 tonnes per day, a 38% rise following supply maintenance orders. The surge aims to offset disruptions in imports from West Asia due to regional conflict. While daily consumption averages 90,000 tonnes, the increase in local output reduces the daily import requirement from 55,000 tonnes to approximately 30,000 tonnes.

articles.headline: LPG Tanker Shivalik diverted from Mangaluru to Dahej Port
articles.summary: The LPG Tanker Shivalik, carrying 26,000 tonnes of fuel, will not reach Mangaluru as scheduled. Sources indicate the vessel is sailing to Dahej in Gujarat to discharge its balance load after stopping at Mundra. This delay further strains the LPG supply chain in Karnataka. Another consignment of 30,000 tonnes is expected at New Mangalore Port from the U.S. by the end of the month.

articles.headline: Karnataka adds 50 new Global Capability Centres in fiscal year 2025
articles.summary: The Karnataka Digital Economy Mission (KDEM) reported the establishment of 40 new GCCs in Bengaluru and 10 in other clusters during FY25. Total GCC talent in the state has grown from 3.5 lakh in 2021 to 6.5 lakh in 2026. The report also highlights that 3,300 startups were registered outside Bengaluru under the 'Beyond Bengaluru' initiative, generating over 5,500 jobs.

articles.headline: West Asia conflict triggers 75% drop in Indian medical tourism
articles.summary: Travel disruptions and airspace restrictions caused by the West Asia conflict have led to a significant decline in international patients visiting India. Delhi's Fortis Healthcare reported a 30% drop in overall footfall, while some hospitals saw a 75% dip in Middle Eastern patients. Flight cancellations from Iraq and redirected routes from Oman have severely impacted elective treatments.

articles.headline: IRDAI proposes Public Insurance Registry to consolidate sector data
articles.summary: The Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (IRDAI) is mooting a Public Insurance Registry (PIR) to improve fraud detection and transparency. The PIR is envisioned as a consent-driven digital infrastructure covering the entire policy lifecycle. It aims to reduce information asymmetry and align with the Bima Sugam e-marketplace for seamless customer service.

articles.headline: Woman killed as 60-foot iron footbridge collapses in North Delhi
articles.summary: A woman in her 50s died after a rusted 60-foot-long footbridge over the Najafgarh drain in Roop Nagar collapsed. Though the bridge was declared structurally unsafe and officially closed in July last year, it continued to be used by the public. Police believe the victim was homeless and fell into the drain during the collapse.

articles.headline: SW Railway postpones promotion exams after protests over Kannada exclusion
articles.summary: South Western Railway postponed departmental promotion exams across Karnataka after protesters demanded the inclusion of Kannada as a language option. Protests by the Karnataka Rakshana Vedike led to the cancellation of tests for 295 posts. CM Siddaramaiah criticized the exclusion of local languages in central government recruitments.

articles.headline: Second LPG shipment reaches Gujarat despite Hormuz tensions
articles.summary: The Indian-flagged tanker MT Nanda Devi arrived at Vadinar, Gujarat, carrying 46,500 metric tonnes of LNG. The cargo will be transferred via a ship-to-ship operation to improve turnaround time. This follows the recent arrival of MT Shivalik at Mundra, underscoring steady energy inflows despite the conflict in West Asia.

articles.headline: FACT announces ₹6,350-crore expansion to boost fertilizer output
articles.summary: Fertilisers and Chemicals Travancore Limited (FACT) plans to invest ₹6,350 crore in its 'Capex 2.0' programme. The initiative includes a new 1,500 MTPD urea plant and phosphoric acid units. The expansion aims to increase annual fertilizer production from 1 million tonnes to 1.5 million tonnes, supporting India's self-reliance goals.

articles.headline: India vehicle scrappage network operating at 25% capacity
articles.summary: Road Transport Minister Nitin Gadkari informed Parliament that while India’s 134 authorized scrappage facilities can dismantle 1.6 million vehicles annually, only 2.8 lakh have been scrapped in FY26. A mismatch between scrap value and resale value for older commercial vehicles remains a major bottleneck, alongside the dominance of the informal recycling sector.

articles.headline: Imported Coal Power Units to Run at Full Capacity
articles.summary: The government may direct 15 imported coal-based power plants to operate at full capacity this summer to meet an expected peak demand of 271 GW. Invoking Section 11 of the Electricity Act 2003, the move aims to ensure grid stability. Plants like Tata Power’s Mundra unit will be allowed to pass through higher fuel costs beyond existing agreements.

articles.headline: Karnataka MP Claims State Shortchanged in Railway Allocations
articles.summary: Congress MP G Kumar Naik told the Lok Sabha that Karnataka continues to receive inadequate railway funding despite regional imbalances. He noted Karnataka's railway density lags behind 13 other states. While Karnataka received an average of Rs 7,600 crore annually, states like Gujarat and Uttar Pradesh were allocated significantly higher amounts of Rs 19,000 crore and Rs 12,500 crore.

articles.headline: Rs 1.65 Trillion EPC Investment Expected in Transmission Infrastructure
articles.summary: India's power transmission sector is projected to attract Rs 1.65 trillion in investments over the next three years, according to Godrej Enterprises Group. This includes Rs 25,000 crore in the substation segment and Rs 14,000 crore for data centers. The growth is driven by rapid renewable capacity additions in states like Gujarat and Rajasthan and industrial demand in Tamil Nadu.

articles.headline: India Post launches 24-hour and 48-hour guaranteed Speed Post services
articles.summary: India Post introduced premium Speed Post services in Bengaluru, guaranteeing next-day delivery (D+1) and two-day delivery (D+2) between six metro cities: Delhi, Mumbai, Bengaluru, Chennai, Kolkata, and Hyderabad. The service utilizes guaranteed cargo space on passenger flights. Customers are offered full postage refunds for delays and OTP-based delivery confirmation.

articles.headline: Delhi government to procure swanky VIP boats for Yamuna inspections
articles.summary: The Delhi government’s irrigation and flood control department has issued a tender for two high-end, air-conditioned boats costing Rs 3.10 crore each. The enclosed-cabin vessels are intended for VVIP/VIP inspections of the Yamuna river. The procurement process is expected to take five months after the bidding concludes on March 19.

articles.headline: Sonam Wangchuk welcomes Ladakh dialogue outreach following jail release
articles.summary: Activist Sonam Wangchuk welcomed the Centre's offer for dialogue after his release from nearly six months of detention under the National Security Act. Addressing a press conference, he called for a 'win-win' resolution for Ladakh’s political demands, including statehood, while reaffirming his faith in democratic institutions.

articles.headline: Siddaramaiah warns departments over unanswered questions in Karnataka Assembly
articles.summary: Karnataka CM Siddaramaiah has issued a warning to ministers and IAS officers after only 90 out of 245 legislative questions were answered. The Chief Secretary issued show-cause notices to seven senior officers. The move follows a walkout by the Speaker, who expressed displeasure at the lack of administrative accountability to the House.

articles.headline: EC orders reshuffle of IAS and IPS officers in Kerala
articles.summary: The Election Commission of India has ordered the immediate transfer of several senior officers in Kerala to ensure impartial assembly polls on April 9. Changes include new district police chiefs and collectors in Kozhikode and Alappuzha. Transferred officers are barred from election-related duties until the process is complete.

articles.headline: AIUDF and AJP release candidate lists for Assam assembly polls
articles.summary: The AIUDF and Assam Jatiya Parishad released their candidate lists for the April 9 Assam assembly elections. AIUDF named 12 candidates in its second list, while AJP released its first list of eight names. The ruling BJP is expected to announce its candidates on Wednesday following seat-sharing talks with allies.

articles.headline: Five Congress MLAs cross-vote in narrow Haryana Rajya Sabha victory
articles.summary: The Congress party narrowly won a Rajya Sabha seat in Haryana by just one vote after five of its legislators allegedly cross-voted or had their votes declared invalid. Despite the internal rebellion, Congress candidate Karamvir Singh Boudh defeated the BJP-backed independent Satish Nandal. BJP member Sanjay Bhatia won the other seat comfortably.

articles.headline: Odisha Congress seeks disqualification of three MLAs for cross-voting
articles.summary: The Odisha Congress has moved the Assembly Speaker to disqualify three MLAs—Sofia Firdous, Dasarathi Gomango, and Ramesh Jena—for allegedly voting against the party line in the Rajya Sabha elections. The party accused them of violating the whip, aiding the victory of a BJP-backed independent candidate.

articles.headline: Speaker revokes suspension of eight Opposition MPs in Lok Sabha
articles.summary: Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla revoked the suspension of eight Opposition MPs following a motion by the Parliamentary Affairs Minister. The members, suspended for unruly conduct, expressed regret for 'inadvertent indiscretions.' The government and opposition agreed to maintain a 'Lakshman Rekha' to ensure the House functions with dignity.

articles.headline: Gujarat panel submits report backing Uniform Civil Code implementation
articles.summary: A high-level committee led by a retired Supreme Court judge submitted a report to the Gujarat CM proposing a Uniform Civil Code. The report recommends a common legal framework for marriage, divorce, and inheritance across all religions. The state government may introduce a draft UCC bill during the current budget session.

articles.headline: Maharashtra legislature passes bill to prohibit forcible religious conversions
articles.summary: The Maharashtra Legislative Council cleared the Freedom of Religion Bill 2026, which mandates prior notification for conversions and imposes up to seven years in prison for those using coercion or fraud. The bill, already passed by the Assembly, aims to stop organized mass conversions. It was supported by Shiv Sena (UBT) but opposed by Congress.

articles.headline: PM Modi and UAE President discuss safety of Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi spoke with UAE President Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan regarding the West Asia conflict. Both leaders emphasized the importance of ensuring free navigation through the Strait of Hormuz. Modi reiterated India's condemnation of attacks on the UAE and discussed the safety of the 4 million Indians living there.

articles.headline: India negotiates safe passage for 2.7 lakh tonnes of LPG
articles.summary: The Indian government is in talks with Iran, the US, and GCC nations to secure the passage of six fully laden LPG carriers currently stranded west of the Strait of Hormuz. Each ship carries approximately 45,000 tonnes of fuel. The Ministry of External Affairs is monitoring the situation closely to ensure critical energy supplies reach Indian ports despite the ongoing regional conflict.

articles.headline: Parliamentary panel recommends formal foreign policy roadmap
articles.summary: A Parliamentary Committee on External Affairs has urged the Ministry to formulate a comprehensive, publicly available document outlining India’s medium-term foreign policy goals. The panel noted that 36 other nations already publish such strategies and argued that the absence of a formal roadmap is a significant gap for a country of India's global stature.

articles.headline: India Condemns Pakistan Attack on Kabul Hospital as Barbaric
articles.summary: India has condemned Pakistan’s airstrike on the Omid Addiction Treatment Hospital in Kabul, which reportedly killed 400 people. The external affairs ministry described the act as a cowardly assault on Afghan sovereignty during the holy month of Ramadan. India reiterated its unwavering support for Afghanistan’s territorial integrity while urging for the accountability of perpetrators.

articles.headline: Sonam Wangchuk Hopes SC Verdict Guides Use of NSA Laws
articles.summary: Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk addressed his first press conference after being released from Jodhpur Jail, following the revocation of National Security Act (NSA) charges. He expressed hope that the Supreme Court would record a judgment in his case to guide future policy makers on the appropriate use of stringent laws. He called for meaningful dialogue with the people of Ladakh.

articles.headline: Himachal Government Withdraws Cabinet Status for Board Heads
articles.summary: In an austerity move, the cash-strapped Himachal Pradesh government has withdrawn Cabinet-rank status for chairmen and advisors of various boards and commissions. The order also defers 20% of their monthly emoluments until September 2026. The state faces a debt of Rs 1.40 lakh crore, and the move aims to reduce protocol-related expenditures and official entitlements.

articles.headline: TMC Drops 75 Sitting MLAs in Bengal Election List
articles.summary: Trinamool Congress chief Mamata Banerjee announced 291 candidates for the upcoming West Bengal assembly polls, dropping 75 sitting legislators due to poor performance. The list includes 52 women and 47 minority candidates. Banerjee will contest from Bhabanipur, facing BJP's Suvendu Adhikari. The party expressed confidence in winning 226 seats to return to power.

articles.headline: Key Ministries May Get Direct Powers for Online Content Takedowns
articles.summary: The central government is considering amendments to Section 69(A) of the IT Act to allow the ministries of I&B, Home, External Affairs, and Defence to issue emergency content-blocking orders. Currently, only the IT Ministry (Meity) holds this power. The move aims to ensure swift action using domain expertise but may increase the volume of blocking orders for social media platforms.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends ‘Golden Share’ for Strategic PSU Control
articles.summary: A parliamentary committee on finance has urged the government to incorporate “golden shares” or indirect control models to safeguard strategic interests if state ownership falls below 51%. The proposal, currently at the ideation stage, would allow the government to maintain veto power over major decisions even with a 26% stake, protecting PSUs from hostile takeovers.

articles.headline: Trinamool Congress Drops 74 Sitting MLAs for West Bengal Polls
articles.summary: TMC chief Mamata Banerjee released a list of 291 candidates for the Bengal Assembly polls, dropping over one-third of sitting legislators to neutralise anti-incumbency. Polling will take place on April 23 and 29. Banerjee will contest from Bhabanipur. The party retained 135 MLAs and fielded 74 new faces, aiming for a fourth successive term with a goal of 226 seats.

articles.headline: Ladakh activist Sonam Wangchuk released after 170 days in detention
articles.summary: Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk was released from Jodhpur Central Jail after the Centre revoked his detention under the National Security Act. Wangchuk was detained following violent clashes in Leh in September 2025. Upon release, he called for judicial closure of his case and urged flexibility in negotiations between the government and Ladakhi leadership over constitutional status.

articles.headline: Bengaluru SEC requests live webcasting from all polling stations
articles.summary: The Karnataka State Election Commission (SEC) has requested the government to provide live webcasting for all polling booths during the upcoming Bengaluru civic elections to ensure transparency. The SEC emphasized the need for proof against tampering as the polls shift back to ballot papers. The Finance Department has currently approved webcasting for only a limited number of booths.

articles.headline: Railway recruitment exams postponed following protests by Kannada groups
articles.summary: South Western Railway deferred departmental recruitment exams for 295 posts after Karnataka Rakshana Vedike members protested the exclusion of Kannada as a medium. Protesters blocked exam centers in Bengaluru and Hubballi, demanding candidates be allowed to write in regional languages. Chief Minister Siddaramaiah criticized the authorities for the sudden cancellation.

articles.headline: BJP alleges rampant corruption in Bengaluru building plan approvals
articles.summary: BJP members representing Bengaluru alleged in the Legislative Assembly that bribes of ₹100 per sq. ft are being collected for apartment construction approvals. Senior member Satish Reddy accused the government of corruption in issuing e-khatas and property tax collection. He also criticized the proposed 17-km tunnel road project, calling it an excessive expenditure of public funds.

articles.headline: India's West Asia policy reset aims for strategic autonomy
articles.summary: Indian foreign policy toward West Asia is undergoing a reset, prioritizing bilateral strategic and security ties with Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) nations and Israel. Prime Minister Modi’s 15 visits to GCC countries reflect growing socio-economic partnerships worth $160 billion. Analysts suggest India is positioning itself as a stable alternative hub amid U.S.-Iran hostilities.

articles.headline: Congress demands all-party meeting on Women’s Reservation Act
articles.summary: Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge requested Prime Minister Narendra Modi to chair an all-party meeting to discuss the implementation roadmap for the Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam. This follows a letter from Parliamentary Affairs Minister Kiren Rijiju seeking a consensus. The Congress maintains that a high-level meeting is necessary to discuss the operationalization of the law.

articles.headline: EC deploys 1,111 observers for upcoming state assembly polls
articles.summary: The Election Commission deployed 1,111 central observers across West Bengal, Assam, Kerala, Puducherry, and Tamil Nadu for the upcoming elections. West Bengal received the highest allocation with over 450 observers. The deployment includes general, police, and expenditure observers tasked with ensuring fair polling. All observers must be stationed in their constituencies by Wednesday.

articles.headline: Fourteen arrested in Varanasi for eating biryani during Iftar on Ganga
articles.summary: UP police arrested 14 men after a video showed them consuming chicken biryani during an Iftar party on a boat in the Ganga river. The action followed a complaint by the BJP Yuva Morcha, alleging the act defiled a place of worship and hurt religious sentiments. The accused were booked under the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita and environmental laws.

articles.headline: Muslim families in Uttam Nagar plan to leave following communal tension
articles.summary: Several families in Hastsal village are planning to temporarily leave the area ahead of Eid due to threats from religious groups following a fatal Holi clash. Residents reported fears after local leaders allegedly threatened violence. Delhi Police stated they are holding meetings with residents to ensure safety and maintain communal harmony.

articles.headline: Minor girl found murdered in abandoned Bengaluru building; boyfriend suspected
articles.summary: A 17-year-old girl was found with her throat slit in an unoccupied building in Bengaluru's Devara Jeevanahalli. Police have launched a manhunt for her 20-year-old boyfriend, the prime suspect. The couple reportedly met frequently in the building, which is owned by the suspect's relatives.

articles.headline: Gadag police arrest five for cheating students with fake scholarships
articles.summary: Five men were arrested in Gadag district for allegedly running a cyber fraud syndicate. The group lured students with promises of financial aid, convincing them to open bank accounts which were then used for illegal transactions. Police seized 11 mobile phones and eight bank kits containing passbooks and ATM cards.

articles.headline: Karnataka legislators urge crackdown on illicit 'CH powder' liquor
articles.summary: MLAs in the Karnataka Assembly raised concerns over the manufacture of illegal liquor using 'CH powder,' particularly in districts bordering Andhra Pradesh. Excise Minister RB Timmapur stated that while 183 cases have been registered, the substance does not currently fall under the NDPS Act, hindering enforcement efforts.

articles.headline: LPG black marketing ring busted in Bidar; four arrested
articles.summary: Police in Bidar arrested four individuals and seized 105 LPG cylinders being sold illegally. The gang reportedly diverted authorized supplies to the black market for higher prices. Two trucks were also confiscated during the raid at a farm in Janwada village.

articles.headline: UP teacher loses Rs 1.57 crore in Meta CEO impersonation scam
articles.summary: A retired teacher in Kanpur was defrauded of Rs 1.57 crore over 13 months after being contacted by scammers impersonating Mark Zuckerberg. The victim was lured into a fake investment partnership to open a school. Police have frozen Rs 30.42 lakh and identified the case as a 'pig butchering' scam involving multiple fake identities.

articles.headline: Indian Navy deploys task forces to escort energy tankers
articles.summary: The Navy has deployed two naval task forces to provide ship-by-ship escort for merchant vessels carrying crude oil and gas through the Strait of Hormuz. Despite US calls for a coalition, India is maintaining 'strategic autonomy' and using behind-the-scenes diplomacy with Iran to ensure the safety of its flagged vessels.

articles.headline: Telangana encourages shift from paddy to palm oil cultivation
articles.summary: The Telangana government is urging farmers to move away from water-intensive paddy toward oil palm, following an 80% rise in palm oil prices over a decade. The state plans to inaugurate a ₹300-crore oil palm factory in Siddhipet on March 22. The goal is to expand the current one lakh hectares of oil palm to 10 lakh hectares in the coming years.

articles.headline: Labor Shortage Looms as Migrant Workers Leave Big Cities
articles.summary: Indian industries and delivery platforms are bracing for a blue-collar worker shortage as migrants return home. The retreat is triggered by LPG shortages making canteens difficult to run, upcoming assembly elections in states like West Bengal and Kerala, and the recent Holi festival. Some firms are offering subsidized food packages to discourage workers from leaving during the crisis.

articles.headline: Akshaya Patra Completes 25 Years; Serves 5 Billionth Mid-Day Meal
articles.summary: The Akshaya Patra Foundation commemorated 25 years of service with President Droupadi Murmu serving its 5 billionth meal. The program feeds 2.35 million children in 2,500 schools daily. The foundation is adopting AI for invoice reconciliation and nutritional studies, aiming to serve 3 million children by 2030 despite rising fuel and commodity costs.

articles.headline: Global markets decline as Iran-Israel-US war drives oil prices up
articles.summary: Global equities have fallen sharply after US and Israeli airstrikes on Iran led to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, pushing crude oil past $100 per barrel. Asian markets have taken the biggest hit, with indices in South Korea, Japan, and India dropping over 6%. Saudi Arabia's market remains an outlier, gaining 4.5% due to domestic capital shifts.

articles.headline: Second LPG tanker reaches India safely after transiting Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: The MT Nanda Devi arrived at an offshore anchorage in Gujarat with 46,500 tonnes of LPG, enough for 3.3 million cylinders. The vessel transited the blockaded Strait of Hormuz following diplomatic efforts. India, which imports over 60% of its LPG, is prioritizing domestic supplies as panic bookings surged due to the West Asian conflict.

articles.headline: RBI injects Rs 48,014 crore to manage liquidity in banking system
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India conducted a seven-day variable rate repo auction to infuse Rs 48,014 crore into the banking system. The move aims to manage transient liquidity tightening caused by advance tax payouts. Banking liquidity surplus dropped sharply from Rs 2.08 lakh crore to approximately Rs 75,484 crore in just one day.

articles.headline: India opposes WTO bid for permanent e-commerce duty moratorium
articles.summary: At the WTO’s 14th Ministerial Conference, India is resisting a US-led proposal to make the moratorium on e-commerce customs duties permanent. India argues the regime disproportionately benefits developed nations while causing developing countries to lose significant revenue—estimated at $500 million annually for India alone. New Delhi seeks a complete rethink of the 1998 agreement.

articles.headline: Fertilizer shortage feared in India ahead of kharif season
articles.summary: The Indian fertilizer industry is concerned about urea supplies for the upcoming kharif season, which accounts for 60% of annual agricultural output. Gas supply disruptions linked to the Iran conflict have pushed production costs higher. While the government is directing units like IFFCO and Kribhco to maximize output, the subsidy burden for FY27 is expected to face upward pressure.

articles.headline: High crude prices could trim India GDP growth by 100 bps
articles.summary: Chief Economic Advisor V. Anantha Nageswaran warned a parliamentary panel that if crude oil remains at $130 per barrel for 2-3 quarters, India's GDP growth could drop by 100 basis points. While growth is sustainable at $90, higher prices could push inflation toward 5.5% and widen the current account deficit to 3.2%. The fiscal deficit could also rise from 4.4% to 5.6%.

articles.headline: LPG crisis triggers migrant labour exodus in South India
articles.summary: A week-long LPG shortage in Tamil Nadu and Kerala has disrupted the hospitality and small industrial sectors. Migrant workers from Assam and West Bengal are returning home early, citing difficulties in managing food costs as commercial fuel supplies dwindle. Restaurant managers report that many employees, originally planning to leave in April for elections, have already departed.

articles.headline: India's external debt remains manageable despite West Asia conflict
articles.summary: A comparative analysis shows India's external sector is more resilient now than during the 1991 or 2013 crises. External debt stood at $746 billion in September 2025, with a debt-to-GDP ratio of 19% (down from 28% in FY91). While forex reserves recently dipped by $12 billion due to the conflict, import cover remains comfortable at 10 months.

articles.headline: India Inc's offshore borrowing crosses $5 billion in January
articles.summary: External Commercial Borrowings (ECBs) by Indian companies reached $5.336 billion in January 2026, the highest this fiscal. Rising domestic bond yields prompted firms like Microsoft India, Exim Bank, and Muthoot Finance to seek cheaper funding overseas. The RBI's liberalized guidelines have further facilitated this surge in offshore debt mobilization.

articles.headline: NPCI expands UPI merchant acceptance in Sri Lanka
articles.summary: NPCI International Payments has reaffirmed its commitment to expanding UPI acceptance for Indian tourists in Sri Lanka. The initiative is designed to support Sri Lanka's digital economy and strengthen bilateral economic ties through seamless cross-border payment experiences.

articles.headline: Global fertilizer prices spike as sulfur supplies squeeze
articles.summary: Shipments of key fertilizer chemicals like sulfur and natural gas are stranded in the Strait of Hormuz due to the Iran war. Urea prices in the Middle East rose 19% in a single week. Experts warn that reduced fertilizer application in South Asia could significantly dent crop yields, threatening regional food security during critical sowing windows.

articles.headline: ISMA seeks sugar price hike as cane dues reach ₹5,000 crore
articles.summary: The Indian Sugar and Bio-energy Manufacturers Association (ISMA) has requested an urgent increase in the minimum selling price of sugar. Sugarcane arrears in Maharashtra alone have hit ₹4,898 crore. Despite higher production this season (26.21 million tonnes so far), ex-mill realizations are lagging behind rising production costs, straining mill cash flows.

articles.headline: Graduate unemployment in India remains high at nearly 40%
articles.summary: A report by Azim Premji University highlights that graduate unemployment for those aged 15-25 is nearly 40%, falling to 20% for the 25-29 age group. While educational enrollment has risen sharply, only a small proportion of graduates secure stable salaried jobs within a year. The report calls for accelerated job creation to leverage the country's demographic dividend.

articles.headline: Plan to Privatise IDBI Bank may Start from Scratch
articles.summary: The Indian government is likely to restart the IDBI Bank privatisation process after financial bids were lower than the reserve price. A panel of ministers will be briefed soon. Officials are re-examining the reserve price mechanism, particularly the reliance on stock prices for banks with limited public float, to avoid market manipulation risks.

articles.headline: LPG Sales Drop 17% in March Amid Gulf War Logistics
articles.summary: State-run oil companies reported a 17% decline in LPG sales for early March as supply chains from the Gulf were disrupted by the Iran war. The government prioritized household supply by curtailing commercial and industrial gas usage. In contrast, petrol and diesel sales rose as consumers rushed to refuel amid fears of future shortages.

articles.headline: FM Sitharaman Says India Can Handle Iran War Shocks
articles.summary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman stated that India’s economic might, built on a decade of prudent policymaking, allows it to effectively tackle West Asian war shocks. She highlighted that capital spending has grown from Rs 2.63 lakh crore in FY18 to Rs 12.20 lakh crore in FY27, creating a high multiplier effect that buffers the nation against external volatility.

articles.headline: New FDI Rules Allow NRI Esops in China and Hong Kong
articles.summary: A subtle change in New Delhi’s FDI policy, Press Note 2 of 2026, allows Indian companies to freely offer Esops to staff in China, Nepal, and Hong Kong. The update removes the restrictive 'situated in' phrase, enabling NRIs and foreign nationals in land-border countries to invest directly in Indian stocks without prior government approval, provided they are not citizens of those nations.

articles.headline: China Overtakes Netherlands as India’s Third-Largest Export Destination in February
articles.summary: China became India’s third-largest export destination in February, surpassing the Netherlands. Exports to China rose 32.4% to $1.67 billion, though overall merchandise exports declined 0.81% year-on-year to $36.61 billion. Shipments to the US and UAE, India’s top two markets, also saw contractions during the month due to tariff uncertainties and logistics bottlenecks.

articles.headline: Government Ramps Up Domestic LPG Output Amid West Asia Crisis
articles.summary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman stated that domestic LPG production has been increased by 38% to maintain supplies as the West Asia conflict disrupts imports. India relies on the region for 90% of its LPG imports. Refineries are diverting propane and butane streams to prioritize cooking gas for households over commercial users as the Strait of Hormuz remains restricted.

articles.headline: Finance Ministry Cuts MeitY Budget Allocation for Next Fiscal Year
articles.summary: The finance ministry reduced the proposed budget for the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) by 17% for FY27. The allocation was cut from a proposed Rs 28,169 crore to Rs 21,632 crore. The reduction follows lower-than-expected expenditure in the first two quarters of the current fiscal, specifically in the PLI scheme and India AI Mission.

articles.headline: LPG Consumption in India Drops 17% Amid West Asia Tensions
articles.summary: India’s LPG consumption fell 17.7% in the first half of March due to supply disruptions linked to the war in West Asia. Consumption dropped to 1.147 million tonnes compared to 1.387 million tonnes a year ago. Despite two tankers carrying 92,712 tonnes arriving at Indian ports, the government remains concerned as 60% of domestic requirements are met via imports through the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.headline: India Builds Rs 600 Crore War Chest for Spot LNG Purchases
articles.summary: The government is creating a fund exceeding Rs 600 crore to augment gas supplies to fertilizer plants through spot markets. The move aims to cushion a potential drop in LNG availability below the assured 70% level if the Iran crisis persists. Urea units rely on LNG for 80% of their raw material costs, and the government aims to prevent a significant production drop.

articles.headline: LPG Shortage Threatens Darjeeling Tea Estates’ First Flush Harvest
articles.summary: Darjeeling tea planters warn that a shortage of commercial LPG could disrupt processing during the crucial first flush harvest. The premium early-season harvest generates 80% of annual revenue for Darjeeling tea, a GI product. Supply hierarchy changes have deprioritized industrial users, raising the risk of factory shutdowns that support 55,000 permanent workers.

articles.headline: Concor Slashes Export Charges to Mitigate Impact of Gulf Crisis
articles.summary: Container Corporation of India (Concor) has reduced storage-related charges and offered a 5% rail freight discount for export-laden containers stuck at its inland depots. The move provides 30 days of free storage for factory-stuffed containers during March. Additionally, Customs will permit temporary unloading and storage of foreign bulk cargo diverted to Indian ports due to security concerns.

articles.headline: India Records Two-Thirds of Unemployed Youth as Graduates in 2023
articles.summary: The State of Working India 2026 report states that 67% of unemployed Indians aged 20-29 were graduates in 2023, up from 46% in 2017. Only 6.7% of all graduates held permanent salaried jobs. The report highlights a tenuous link between vocational training and manufacturing firm locations, noting that institutional quality in private ITIs has declined despite a 300% increase in numbers.

articles.headline: Rajasthan to Begin Mustard and Gram Procurement at MSP
articles.summary: Rajasthan State Cooperative Marketing Federation (RAJFED) will start procuring mustard and gram at MSP from March 25. The Centre has declared an MSP of Rs 6,200 per quintal for mustard and Rs 5,875 for gram. The state has set procurement limits of 1.37 million tonnes for mustard. Farmers can register through biometric identification or QR code scanning.

articles.headline: Indian Stock Markets Advance for Second Session; Sensex Rises 568 Points
articles.summary: The Sensex rose 0.75% to close at 76,071 while the Nifty 50 gained 172 points to settle at 23,581 on Tuesday. Metal and Realty stocks led the gains, rising 2.8% and 1.8% respectively. Markets recovered despite Brent crude holding firm above $100 per barrel. Foreign investors have sold Indian equities worth over $7 billion so far in March.

articles.headline: Foreign Investors Pull Rs 1.37 Trillion From Indian Equities
articles.summary: FPIs have withdrawn a net Rs 1.37 trillion from Indian equities in FY26 due to geopolitical risks and currency volatility. Conversely, the debt segment saw modest inflows of Rs 13,964 crore, driven by attractive domestic yields and hopes for inclusion in the Bloomberg bond index. Yields on benchmark 10-year government bonds rose by 14 bps during the period.

articles.headline: Vegetable prices crash in Bengaluru as LPG crisis hits bulk buyers
articles.summary: Vegetable prices in Bengaluru, including cauliflower and capsicum, have dropped by up to 40% as an LPG shortage forces small eateries and street-side food outlets to shut down. Traders report a glut in supply during what is typically a peak festive period for Ugadi. Conversely, flower prices have surged, with jasmine and crossandra touching ₹1,000 per kg due to high demand.

articles.headline: Niti Aayog and Planning Ministry rapped for poor financial planning
articles.summary: A Parliamentary Standing Committee on Finance criticized the Ministry of Planning and Niti Aayog for significant under-utilization of allocated funds. In fiscal years 2023-24 and 2024-25, actual spending was only 35% and 33% of the budget estimates. The committee called for more realistic planning to avoid the 'disproportionate rush' to exhaust funds in the final quarter.

articles.headline: British spy novelist and Harry Palmer creator Len Deighton dies
articles.summary: Acclaimed writer Len Deighton has died at the age of 97. Famous for 'The IPCRESS File,' Deighton introduced a gritty, working-class alternative to the James Bond archetype during the 1960s. His literary career included the Bernard Samson trilogies and several military history books, leaving a significant mark on the espionage thriller genre.

articles.headline: Karnataka Women Commission seeks action against 'obscene' song lyrics
articles.summary: The Karnataka State Women Commission has written to the CBFC demanding strict action against derogatory and obscene lyrics in the song 'Sarke Chunar Teri Sarke' from the film 'KD: The Devil'. The panel stated the lyrics portray women in a derogatory manner and violate standards of public decency.

articles.headline: Heavy rain and hailstorm disrupt life in North Karnataka
articles.summary: Parts of North Karnataka, including Belagavi, Dharwad, and Hubballi, witnessed heavy rainfall and hailstorms, providing relief from heat but disrupting daily activities. In Belagavi, a hailstorm lasted over an hour, affecting traffic and local businesses. The state capital, Bengaluru, also received light rainfall.

articles.headline: Sudden hailstorms and rain surprise residents in parts of Karnataka
articles.summary: Bengaluru and several Karnataka districts, including Dharwad and Hassan, received unexpected pre-monsoon showers and hailstorms. In Dharwad, hailstones covered roads and farmland, resembling snow. The IMD predicts light rainfall will continue in Bengaluru for the next three days, bringing respite from rising temperatures. Trace rainfall was recorded at major city weather stations.

articles.headline: MeitY warns social media firms to strictly control harmful AI content
articles.summary: The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology has issued a notice to online platforms to curb illegal AI-generated content. Intermediaries were warned that failure to act could lead to the loss of 'safe harbour' protections and criminal liability. Platforms must inform users about the consequences of sharing deepfakes and harmful AI media.

articles.headline: Jio Payments Bank introduces UPI-based cash withdrawal
articles.summary: Jio Payments Bank has launched a feature allowing customers to withdraw cash by scanning a UPI QR code at its business correspondent touchpoints. The service aims to enhance digital banking accessibility through the Jio Financial Services ecosystem.

articles.headline: Amara Raja Design develops indigenous power cabinets for Navy
articles.summary: Amara Raja Design Alpha has developed a power conditioning cabinet for naval sonar systems, achieving 61% indigenization. The system distributions support loads up to 160 kW with high reliability for maritime operations. The development, conducted in Kochi, is expected to provide 40% cost efficiencies compared to imported alternatives.

articles.headline: Yotta Data Services to deploy 5,000 Nvidia GPUs for AI
articles.summary: Yotta has signed a deal with Gorilla Technology Group to deploy approximately 640 Nvidia HGX B200 servers at its Navi Mumbai data center. The agreement, valued at $500 million over five years, will support AI compute demands in India and the Global South. This collaboration is part of India’s broader sovereign AI infrastructure buildout.

articles.headline: NPCI Targets Hindi Heartland and North East for UPI Growth
articles.summary: With UPI growth moderating, the National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI) is pushing fintech firms to focus on Uttar Pradesh, Rajasthan, Madhya Pradesh, and North Eastern states. Growth is projected to drop to 25% in FY26 from 82% in FY24. NPCI aims to drive the next phase of adoption in regions with currently low digital payment penetration.

articles.headline: Samtel Avionics Diversifies into Drone and Space Technology
articles.summary: Indian defence firm Samtel is expanding into drone manufacturing and space technology with a budget of over Rs 200 crore. The company, known for its avionics in military helicopters, has created a new division for long-endurance drones and low earth orbit satellites. It is targeting a Rs 1,000-crore order book driven by domestic and global demand for unmanned systems.

articles.headline: Over 100 VCs Queue for Government’s Rs 1Lcr RDI Fund
articles.summary: More than 100 venture capital firms, including Blume Ventures and Chiratae, have applied for the government’s Rs 1 lakh crore Research, Development and Innovation fund. The fund aims to catalyze private investment in deeptech and R&D-focused startups over six years. Successful applicants will receive support in the form of low-interest loans or equity to boost high-tech innovation.

articles.headline: Udaan to Reverse Flip Domicile from Singapore to India
articles.summary: B2B e-commerce platform Udaan plans to move its domicile from Singapore to India in the coming weeks. CEO Vaibhav Gupta stated that a reverse merger with its Indian entity is part of the preparation for an IPO expected in 9-18 months. The company aims to achieve business break-even by mid-next year as it consolidates its technology and wholesale trading units.

articles.headline: OpenAI Pilots Advertising Inside ChatGPT Conversations
articles.summary: OpenAI is testing advertisements within ChatGPT conversations, potentially disrupting the traditional search-based discovery model. The pilot in the US involves adtech partner Criteo and allows brands to appear in response to user prompts. This shift forces agencies to move from search engine optimization to conversational optimization as AI assistants become primary interfaces for discovery.

articles.headline: Apple Surpasses Five-Year PLI Production Target by 80%
articles.summary: Apple Inc overshot its five-year cumulative production target for iPhones under India’s PLI scheme by 80%. Assembled iPhones reached a value of Rs 6.02 trillion against a target of Rs 3.35 trillion. India is expected to account for 30% of global iPhone production by FY26, driven by a massive surge in exports to the US and new factories by Tata Electronics and Foxconn.

articles.headline: Infosys Mandates AI Tool Usage for Managers to Boost Efficiency
articles.summary: Infosys has asked managers at level 6 and above to actively deploy AI tools in client engagements to drive volume growth and productivity. The move comes amid a demand slowdown in the IT sector. The company has structured its workforce into three AI capability tiers: AI-aware, AI builders, and AI masters. Competitors like TCS and Tech Mahindra are also pushing for leadership-level AI usage.

articles.headline: Emergence to Set Up Autonomous AI Research Lab in Bengaluru
articles.summary: New York-based frontier AI firm Emergence plans to hire 500 scientists and engineers over four years for its new India Labs unit. The hub will focus on developing autonomous AI agents capable of operating mission-critical digital public systems, financial networks, and physical infrastructure. The initiative aims to position India beyond traditional IT services.

articles.headline: Agnit Semiconductor Targets 100,000 Units Production in Two Years
articles.summary: Deeptech startup Agnit Semiconductor, incubated at IISc Bangalore, aims to scale its Gallium Nitride (GaN) chip production to 100,000 units over 24 months. The firm raised $2.6 million in seed-extension funding. Agnit focuses on high-efficiency power semiconductors for telecom infrastructure and expects to begin commercial shipments of its first strategic products by July 2027.

articles.headline: Nvidia Predicts $1 Trillion in AI Processor Sales by 2027
articles.summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang unveiled the Blackwell and Rubin chips at the GTC conference, forecasting $1 trillion in AI hardware sales through 2027. Huang stated that computing demand has grown 1 million times in two years. The company also announced partnerships with IBM, Adobe, and Uber, alongside plans for software-driven autonomous vehicle fleets by 2028.

articles.headline: OpenAI Signs Deal to Sell AI to US Defense Agencies
articles.summary: OpenAI will sell access to its AI models to US defense and government agencies through Amazon’s cloud unit. The deal enables OpenAI to support the Pentagon following the agency’s shift from previous provider Anthropic. The contract covers both classified and unclassified work, with the Pentagon having previously secured a $200 million AI supply deal.

articles.headline: Samsung Electronics to Stop Sales of TriFold Foldable Smartphone
articles.summary: Samsung is winding down sales of its $2,899 Galaxy Z TriFold smartphone just three months after its launch. The dual-hinge device, which unfolds into a 10-inch tablet, was intended as a technological showcase rather than a mass-market model. Sales will halt in Korea and the US once existing inventory is cleared, with the product already listed as “sold out” on its website.

articles.headline: Narayana Health completes drone medical logistics pilot in Bengaluru
articles.summary: Narayana Health and Airbound successfully tested an aerial logistics route connecting a Chandapura clinic to Narayana Health City’s central laboratory. The four-km drone flight takes 10 minutes, significantly faster than road transport. The service, operating since January 2026, aims to streamline medical sample transport in the tech corridor.

articles.headline: CBSE to begin registrations for second Class 10 board exam
articles.summary: The CBSE announced that it will start the submission of the List of Candidates for the second Class 10 board examination from Wednesday. The exam, scheduled for May 2026, will follow the same syllabus as the first. This provides students an additional opportunity to improve their scores under the new academic framework.

articles.headline: NMC slaps one crore penalty on Bengaluru medical college
articles.summary: The National Medical Commission (NMC) imposed a ₹1 crore fine on Akash Institute of Medical Sciences for non-disclosure and non-payment of stipends to MBBS interns and PG residents. Seven colleges nationally faced similar penalties for failing to comply with transparency regulations. Continued non-compliance could lead to suspension of admissions and further disciplinary measures.

articles.headline: Abhishek Sharma eyes resilience after mixed T20 World Cup performance
articles.summary: India opener Abhishek Sharma spoke to HT about his 'tournament of extremes' following India's T20 World Cup victory. Despite starting with three consecutive ducks, he credited mentor Yuvraj Singh and captain Suryakumar Yadav for helping him regain focus. His 21-ball fifty in the final against New Zealand proved crucial to the team's success.
