articles.headline: Devon Conway leads New Zealand to T20 victory over South Africa
articles.summary: Devon Conway scored 60 off 49 balls to help New Zealand beat South Africa by 68 runs, leveling the five-match series 1-1. Chasing 176, the Proteas were dismissed for 107 as Ben Sears and Lockie Ferguson took three wickets each. Both teams are currently fielding second-tier lineups following the conclusion of the World Cup.

articles.headline: KKR pacer Harshit Rana likely to miss most of IPL season
articles.summary: Kolkata Knight Riders bowler Harshit Rana is set for a lengthy spell on the sidelines following knee surgery. Rana sustained a ligament strain during a World Cup warm-up match. His absence is a major blow for KKR, who are already missing Mustafizur Rahman due to geopolitical tensions and Matheesha Pathirana due to injury.

articles.headline: India and Pakistan placed in same Hockey World Cup group
articles.summary: The draw for the 2026 Hockey World Cup has placed India and Pakistan in Group D alongside England and Wales. The tournament will be co-hosted by Belgium and the Netherlands in August. The Indian women's team is also in Group D with China, England, and South Africa for their respective tournament.

articles.headline: WADA Rules Could Bar Trump from 2026 World Cup
articles.summary: The World Anti-Doping Agency is considering a rule to bar US government officials from major international sporting events, including the 2026 World Cup and 2028 LA Olympics. The move stems from the US withholding $7.3 million in dues in protest of WADA’s handling of Chinese swimming doping cases. WADA decision-makers may formally adopt the recommendation this November.

articles.headline: RBI women and PSPB men clinch National Table Tennis titles
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) women’s team secured its third title in four years by defeating PSPB 3-2 in a thrilling final. Sreeja Akula won the deciding match against Yashaswini Ghorpade. In the men’s category, the Petroleum Sports Promotion Board (PSPB) clinched its 24th consecutive title with a 3-0 victory over West Bengal in Indore.

articles.headline: Narayana Health completes drone pilot for medical logistics
articles.summary: Narayana Health and Airbound completed a 54-day drone pilot in Bengaluru, logging 700 flights for diagnostic sample transport. The 4 km aerial route takes 10 minutes, significantly faster than road transport. Founder Devi Prasad Shetty stated that centralizing lab testing via drone transport could reduce diagnostic costs by up to 50% through improved automation.

articles.headline: Bharat Biotech Developing Vaccines for Drug-Resistant Superbugs
articles.summary: Bharat Biotech is working on a novel vaccine strategy to combat antimicrobial resistance (AMR), which causes nearly five million deaths globally each year. The company aims to vaccinate patients before elective surgeries to prevent hospital-acquired infections. Other pipeline products include vaccines for tuberculosis, Chikungunya, and diarrheal pathogens, fueling its next growth phase.

articles.headline: Zydus and Lupin Ink Deal for Generic Semaglutide
articles.summary: Zydus Lifesciences and Lupin have signed a licensing pact to co-market a generic version of the weight-loss drug semaglutide in India. The innovate molecule's patent expires on March 21. Lupin will have semi-exclusive rights to market the drug under the brands Semanext and Livarise, paying Zydus upfront fees and milestone payments for the partnership.

articles.headline: Novo Nordisk Resists Price War as Semaglutide Patent Expiry nears
articles.summary: Novo Nordisk plans to maintain its pricing strategy for the obesity drug semaglutide despite the entry of low-cost generic rivals following its patent expiry on March 20. While generic versions are expected to offer 60-70% discounts, Novo will focus on scientific evidence and manufacturing quality. Major domestic players like Sun Pharma and Zydus are preparing to enter the market.

articles.headline: India’s Drug Advisory Board Mandates Boxed Warnings for Morning-After Pills
articles.summary: The Drug Technical Advisory Board (DTAB) has directed manufacturers of emergency contraceptives like i-pill to include boxed warnings on packaging. The labels must state that the pills do not protect against HIV or STIs and should not be used more than twice a month. The move follows concerns over irrational usage and a lack of medical consultation for these drugs.

articles.headline: Redeployment of specialist doctors aimed at reducing neonatal deaths
articles.summary: Health Minister Dinesh Gundu Rao defended the redeployment of gynaecologists and anaesthetists from rural community health centres to taluk hospitals. He stated the move follows an audit showing taluk hospitals need round-the-clock specialist availability to lower the neonatal mortality rate, which has decreased from 62 to 57. The government aims for a target rate of 38 by 2028.

articles.headline: Prison health crisis in West Bengal linked to overcrowding
articles.summary: A health crisis at Jalpaiguri Central Correctional Home killed seven inmates after a herpes simplex virus (HSV) outbreak. The facility’s 171% occupancy rate hindered isolation and hygiene. Experts warn that prison populations in India are five times more likely to develop tuberculosis. The India Justice Report flags a 43% vacancy rate for medical officers in correctional facilities.

articles.headline: Cholesterol in nuclear membranes found to drive melanoma cancer spread
articles.summary: A study by the U.S. National Institutes of Health discovered that high cholesterol levels in the nuclear envelope make cell nuclei 'squishy' and fragile, helping melanoma spread. High levels of the lamin B receptor (LBR) protein trigger this cholesterol production. The finding explains why statins may slow cancer progression and identifies LBR as a potential drug target.

articles.headline: BRICS nations expand cooperation in science and technology innovation
articles.summary: The BRICS+ grouping is deepening scientific partnerships in areas like AI, biotechnology, and climate tech. Under India’s upcoming 2026 presidency, the group aims to address digital divides and public health crises. However, experts suggest the current framework needs a permanent secretariat, similar to the EU’s Horizon program, to manage funding and long-term mega-science projects.

articles.headline: Israel Kills Iran’s Security Chief Ali Larijani and Basij Commander
articles.summary: Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz announced the killing of Iran’s security chief Ali Larijani and Basij militia leader Gholamreza Soleimani in overnight airstrikes. The conflict, now in its third week with 2,000 casualties, has impacted global energy prices. Meanwhile, new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei rejected all de-escalation offers, demanding the US and Israel accept defeat.

articles.headline: US Counter-Terrorism Chief Joe Kent Resigns Over Iran War Concerns
articles.summary: Joe Kent, Director of the National Counterterrorism Center, resigned, stating he cannot support the Trump administration's war in Iran. Kent alleged the conflict was driven by pressure from Israel and that Iran posed no imminent threat. President Trump labeled Kent "weak on security," while Senate Democrats voiced concerns regarding the lack of credible evidence for the ongoing war.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Grants 12 Weeks' Maternity Leave for All Adoptive Mothers
articles.summary: The Supreme Court struck down a provision in the Code on Social Security, 2020, that restricted maternity leave to mothers adopting children under three months old. Ruling that maternity leave is a basic human right, the court awarded 12 weeks of paid leave to all new adoptive mothers regardless of the child's age, emphasizing that emotional bonding requires time for all families.

articles.headline: NCLT Approves Adani Group's ₹14,535 Crore Bid for Jaiprakash Associates
articles.summary: The NCLT approved Adani Enterprises’ ₹14,535 crore resolution plan to acquire Jaiprakash Associates (JAL), outbidding Vedanta and Dalmia Bharat. The deal includes a portfolio of cement, power, real estate, and hospitality assets. The plan involves delisting JAL and cancelling existing shares with nil consideration for current shareholders, with Adani taking control within a 90-day window.

articles.headline: SEBI Proposes Default Nomination for Demat Accounts and Mutual Funds
articles.summary: SEBI has proposed making nomination the default option for new demat accounts and mutual fund folios to reduce onboarding friction. Investors would only need to provide a name and relationship, with additional details being optional. The proposal caps the number of nominees at four and requires an explicit opt-out for those wishing to skip nomination, aiming to reduce operational complexity.

articles.headline: Tata Steel to Infuse $2 Billion Into Singapore Arm, Merging NINL
articles.summary: Tata Steel’s board approved a $2 billion (₹18,488 crore) investment in its Singapore-based subsidiary, T Steel Holdings Pte, starting in FY27 to support operations, capex, and debt repayment. Additionally, the company will merge its wholly owned subsidiary Neelachal Ispat Nigam Ltd (NINL) with itself to consolidate long products assets and achieve operational synergies.

articles.headline: Maruti Suzuki to Challenge ₹5,786 Crore Draft Income Tax Assessment Order
articles.summary: Maruti Suzuki India has received a draft assessment order for FY 2022-23 from income tax authorities proposing disallowances totaling ₹5,786.4 crore. The carmaker plans to challenge the order before the dispute resolution panel and clarified that there is no immediate impact on its financial or operational activities. Despite the announcement, the company's shares rose by 1.88%.

articles.headline: US Sanctions Cloud Future of India’s Strategic Chabahar Port Project
articles.summary: A parliamentary committee warned that the future of the India-backed Chabahar Port in Iran is uncertain following the lapse of US sanctions waivers. While India has fulfilled its $120 million commitment for port equipment and allocated ₹400 crore for FY26, no funds were sought for FY27. The panel urged the external affairs ministry to continue diplomatic efforts to mitigate the impact.

articles.headline: TMC Names 291 Candidates for West Bengal Polls, Drops 74 MLAs
articles.summary: Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee announced 291 Trinamool Congress candidates for the West Bengal assembly elections, dropping 74 sitting MLAs to counter anti-incumbency. Banerjee will seek re-election from Bhabanipur. The list includes up to 55 women and 47 minority candidates. Banerjee also criticized the Election Commission for the ongoing transfers of top police officials in the state.

articles.headline: Congress Releases First List of 55 Candidates for Kerala Assembly Elections
articles.summary: The Congress-led UDF released its first list of 55 candidates for the 140-member Kerala Assembly elections on April 9. The list renominates sitting lawmakers including V.D. Satheesan and Ramesh Chennithala, while dropping one facing sexual assault charges. Partner IUML also named 25 candidates, including two women for the first time, as the alliance seeks to unseat the LDF government.

articles.headline: Centre Hikes Procurement Commission for Grain Agents in North India
articles.summary: The Central government has increased the commission payable to 'arthiyas' (agents) and cooperatives for procuring wheat and paddy in Punjab, Haryana, and Rajasthan. The wheat commission will rise from ₹46 to ₹50.75 per quintal, effective from the 2026-27 rabi season. The revision follows state requests and aims to maintain efficiency in the grain procurement system across major states.

articles.headline: Iran Seeks to Move 2026 World Cup Matches to Mexico
articles.summary: Iran’s football federation is negotiating with FIFA to relocate its 2026 World Cup group matches from the US to Mexico due to player safety concerns following airstrikes by the US and Israel. Iran, scheduled to play in Los Angeles and Seattle, has warned it will not travel to America if safety cannot be guaranteed. FIFA is currently in contact with the participating associations.

articles.headline: Meenakshi Goyat Upsets Antim Panghal in Asian Wrestling Championship Trials
articles.summary: Meenakshi Goyat secured a major upset by defeating two-time World medalist Antim Panghal 6-2 in the 53kg final at the Asian Wrestling Championship trials. Other winners booking their spots for the event include Aman Sehrawat (61kg), Ankit (65kg), Neelam Sirohi (50kg), and Mansi Ahlawat (62kg). Meenakshi used a tactical defensive approach to secure her victory in the final seconds.
